license_note: Confirmed via CrossRef DOI metadata: dx.doi.org/10.17504/protocols.io.rbjd2kn
title: Symbiodinium-enriched RNA extraction from Aiptasia holobiont
author: Anthony Bellantuono

steps_raw.instruction: Freeze anemones in minimal seawater in 2 ml cryotubes in liquid nitrogen and store at -80°C till extraction.
Add 500 ul of host lysis buffer (with 2-mercaptoethanol) to tube containing the frozen anemone and flicked till frozen tissue is dislodged. Transfer to a 1.5 ml centrifuge tube by decanting.
Gently homogenize tissue with a plastic pestle. Vortex at maximum speed for 1 minute. Centrifuge homogenate at 10,000 g for 3 min. Remove and discard supernatant, leaving the symbiont pellet intact.
Resuspend pellet in 1.4 ml of host lysis buffer and vortex till homogenized. Centrifuge suspension at 3,000 g for 3 min.
Discard clear supernatant and add 1.4 ml of host lysis buffer. Vortexed at max till homogenous. Centrifuge at 3,000 g for 3 minutes and discard clear supernatant.

Resuspend Symbiodinium pellet in 600 ul Buffer RLT (Qiagen), transfer to a screw top 2.0 ml tube, and bead beat for 1 min at 6 m/s with approximately 100 μl glass beads. (Beads can be added to tubes and autoclaved in advance.)

Centrifuge the lysate for 3 min at 14000 g. Carefully remove the cleared lysate supernatant by pipetting, and transfer it to a new microcentrifuge tube. Process the cleared lysate with the Qiagen RNeasy Mini Kit as per manufacturer instructions (with on-column DNAse treatment), beginning with the addition of 1 volume of 70% ethanol prior to loading the sample on the supplied columns. In order to eliminate salt contamination in the final sample, users may perform an additional RPE Buffer wash and/or 80% ethanol wash prior to elution of total RNA.
